title: NoSQL Consistency Models Quiz
description: Test your fundamental knowledge about consistency models in distributed NoSQL databases.

questions.question: What is 'Strong Consistency' in a distributed NoSQL database?
options: A way to backup the database.
A guarantee that every read operation receives the most recent write, regardless of which node is accessed.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: What is 'Eventual Consistency' and why is it common in NoSQL?
options: Data is propagated through the cluster over time; for a short window, reads from different nodes might return stale data. This trades consistency for massive availability and performance.
To automatically generate a backup of the database.

questions.question: Which of these consistency models ensures that a user will ALWAYs see the result of their own updates immediately, even if others don't see them yet?
In a distributed NoSQL cluster, which of the following 'Read Preferences' in MongoDB provides the strongest consistency?
What is the primary role of the 'CAP Theorem' in understanding NoSQL consistency models?
options: It's a way to encrypt files using a secret key.
It states that a distributed system can only provide two out of Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
